Support tables for HTML5, CSS3, etc","url":"https://caniuse.com/mdn-css_properties_grid-template-rows_masonry"},{"title":"Making a Masonry Layout That Works Today | CSS-Tricks","url":"https://css-tricks.com/making-a-masonry-layout-that-works-today/"},{"title":"masonry - Modern CSS Daily","url":"https://modern-css.davecross.co.uk/2026/06/19/masonry/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what you need to know about CSS masonry layout browser support in 2025-2026:

## Current Browser Support Status

No browser ships masonry layout unflagged in stable releases as of mid-2025. However, progress is being made:

- **Safari**: CSS Grid Lanes is available in Safari 26 (first to ship) as of early 2026, marking the first browser to ship stable support.

- **Chrome & Edge**: CSS masonry is ready for early developer testing from Chrome and Edge 140. Chrome and Firefox have the feature behind experimental flags and are expected to ship stable support later in 2026.

- **Firefox**: Firefox has had the longest-running flag-gated support, dating back to Firefox 77.

## Syntax and Standardization

The naming and syntax have evolved. The specification settled on the grid-lanes keyword rather than the earlier masonry proposal after years of debate between browser vendors. To create the most common masonry layout where the columns are laid out in a grid, and the rows stack like masonry, use display: grid-lanes along with grid-template-columns.

## Production Readiness

As of early 2026, browser support remains experimental and inconsistent across engines. A progressive enhancement strategy using @supports lets you experiment safely while falling back to standard Grid.
